nodes.label: National Incident Management System (NIMS)
nodes.description: Consistent nationwide template for incident management across all levels of government and organizations

attributes.scope: Federal, state, local, tribal, private-sector, NGOs

nodes.label: Incident Command System (ICS)
nodes.description: Standardized organizational structure for incident management

nodes.label: Incident Commander
nodes.description: Person responsible for overall incident management

nodes.label: Safety Officer
nodes.description: Monitors safety conditions and has authority to stop unsafe operations

nodes.label: Police Department
nodes.description: Coordinates on-site and off-site response once major incident declared

nodes.label: Fire Department
nodes.description: Responsible for fire-related on-site response and technical rescue

nodes.label: Emergency Medical Services
nodes.description: Coordinates medical response, casualty distribution, and hospital notification

nodes.label: Major Incident
nodes.description: Incident requiring special or non-routine arrangements from emergency services
indicators: Involvement of large numbers of people
Need for combined resources of multiple emergency services
Threat of death, serious injury, or homelessness

nodes.label: Minor Incident
nodes.description: Simple undesired event managed through normal service delivery

nodes.label: Incident Command Post (ICP)
nodes.description: Designated on-site facility for incident management

nodes.label: Triage Area
nodes.description: Location for sorting and prioritizing casualties

nodes.label: Evacuation Procedures
nodes.description: Methods for safely removing people from hazardous areas

nodes.label: Cordons
nodes.description: Perimeter controls for access management during incidents

nodes.label: Risk Assessment
nodes.description: Process and document estimating hazard impact and mitigation

nodes.label: Emergency Operations Plan (EOP)
nodes.description: Jurisdiction's plan assigning responsibilities for emergency response

edges.description: NIMS includes ICS as standardized structure
ICS Command led by Incident Commander
Safety Officer is part of Command Staff
Police coordinate response to major incidents
Fire responds to fires and technical rescue
EMS manages triage and casualty distribution
Risk assessment informs emergency operations planning
Minor incidents can escalate to major if not managed

query_patterns.pattern: What agencies are involved in major incident response?
What are the components of ICS?
What procedures are activated during a major incident?
